feedback_message: The field yields a higher output when you give it the nutrients of the colours that aren't involved. E.g., for a blue field, give it yellow and red nutrients. For an orange plant, give it blue nutrients (as red and yellow equal orange).

The field yields a higher output when you try to match the colour of the flower to the ingredients that would make that colour. If the colour is the same as a single ingredient, doubling that ingredient will make a higher score. Red seems to be best as a single.
The field yields a higher output when you match the colours to the colours of the flowers
The field yields a higher output when i even out all the nutrient number

The field yields a higher output when you mix the nutrient colours that produce the colour of the plant. For example: 1x Red and 1x Blue = Purple. The plants that match one of the nutrient colours will only need that coulour of nutrient. For example a blue pant will require 1x blue nutrient. Always use the full 16 nutrients
The field yields a higher output when purple uses all 3, blue - blue etc, if you're left with one nutrient but you need 2 use red instead of yellow it gives more points, averaging 850ish by the end
The field yields a higher output when it gets the red nutrient.
The field yields a higher output when the nutrients added aren't used to mix the primary colour in question
The field yields a higher output when all of the 16 nutrients are used and there are 2 nutrients to each plant, although there will be two plants that only have 1 nutrient each

The field yields a higher output when you use both the blue and yellow colours. Roughly do 1 blue and 1 yellow for each plant. Please avoid using the red completely as this will likely result in a lower score.
The field yields a higher output when sorry, I really couldn't figure out the pattern for success, this was my best solution but it still wasn't great, sorry again.

The field yields a higher output when you complete all the flowers. If you still have a solution left, it's best to use it on the flowers that are mixed (i.e. green = blue + yellow, purple= blue + red). Also focus on the flowers that occur once.

The field yields a higher output when the nutrients you add mix to make the colour of the shape. For example blue and red makes purple, so add a blue and a red nutrient. Adding an extra yellow to the green shape scored higher output.
The field yields a higher output when nutrients are related to flower

The field yields a higher output when using a colour that is the most unrelated to the element's own colour (ie - blue is highest with yellow, red is highest with blue). Whichever colour is furthest away from it on the spectrum yields a higher result.
The field yields a higher output when you move fast and submit faster
The field yields a higher output when you include more of blue manure

The field yields a higher output when you match the colours according to colour theory. For example, for green, add one blue and one yellow. For purple, add one red and one blue. and so forth. For the already existing primary colours e.g. red, blue or yellow, just add one of the same colour. I got a higher score when I had one nutrient left after following these rules and used that to put 2 yellow on the plant with a big yellow square.

The field yields a higher output when - well I'm not certain but I think what matters is mixing the correct colours that make the colours that are not primary & using the nutrients of the primary colours on the plants of secondary colours. So purple is red & blue, green is blue & yellow, & I assume orange is red & yellow although I do not know what the real proportions would be. My highest score was on the first round, when I did equal mixing to make the secondary colours & also gavea drop of blue to blue, yellow to yellow & red to red. Good luck :)
The field yields a higher output when I use fewer bottles of solution
The field yields a higher output when you match the colour combination of the nutrients with the colour of the flower. For example, blue and red combine to give purple.
The field yields a higher output when you use less yellow on orange squares
The field yields a higher output when the drops are spread out more evenly.
The field yields a higher output when you don't add same nutriennt color to the floor that has the same color as the nutrient
The field yields a higher output when just yellow

The field yields a higher output when colours of the nutrients correspond with the colours of the flowers (however, this approach only uses 15 nutrients so unclear if there's a better way that uses 16 nutrients).

The field yields a higher output when you mix the nutrients according to the plant's colour. for plants with a single nutrient color choose that single nutrient otherwise mix to get around that colored plant.
The field yields a higher output when you mix up the nutrients.
The field yields a higher output when you match the potions to the colours. E.g. blue = blue, orange = yellow and red.
The field yields a higher output when you guess the colours needed to get main colour
The field yields a higher output when you mix together the primary colours to make the correct flower colour (e.g., Red + Yellow = Orange)

The field yields a higher output when using a single colour solution per square. So far the highest score came from using a single yellow drop on every square. Mixing the solutions to make the colour of the flower resulted in a lower score.
The field yields a higher output when you choose colours that when mixed make that colour, for example red and yellow make orange, red and blue make purple, and mixing blue and yellow makes green

The field yields a higher output when you spread the nutrients evenly based on a combination of colors that create the color in the center. This maximized my points around the 500 level, however, I couldn't identify any correlation between shape or position within 6 trials, and even this is tenuous at best... but hopefully, it will be useful!
The field yields a higher output when you mix the nutrients to make that colour e.g. blue and red make purple. Not if you repeat the pattern though.

The field yields a higher output when you match the nutrient colours to create the colour of the plant (eg., Blue and yellow to make green, blue and red to make purple, and yellow and red to make orange)
The field yields a higher output when the inital colour is darker than the colour you change it with (say the primary colours - that is blue, yellow and red - a lighter primary colour must be used

The field yields a higher output when there is an equal amount of feed on each plant I think; it's somewhat random though, but make sure you have at least 1 drop on each square. I also tested it via colour mixing, so if it was a green plant I would have 1 drop of blue and one drop of yellow = green. For some reason this paid out more at the start of the rounds from what I could tell. Again it could be completely random though.

The field yields a higher output when distributing the nutrients crresponding to the colours of each flower.For instance, I gave the orange flowers two nutrients corresponding to the sum of the colour (red + yellow makes orange). With the leftover nutrients, I gave one nutrient corresponding to the colour of the flower, and from there, gave one extra to any one flower displaying a primary colour.

The field yields a higher output when one of the nutrients given is one of the colours involved in producing the colour of the flower. When only one colour is chosen instead of both (eg. two red nutrients for an orange flower instead of one red and one yellow nutrient), a higher score is given. When all of the nutrients given are the same colour, a lower output is yielded, but not by much.
The field yields a higher output when you add more of one colour to a plant that doesnt have the nutrient colour and you have to use multiple colours to make it.
The field yields a higher output when you do not use all of what is available. Make sure every one has atleast 1 on either blue, yellow or red.
The field yields a higher output when giving nutrients that create the plants colour, for example mising red and blue for the purple plant.
The field yields a higher output when nutrients used are the same as the colour of the flower for example use 1 blue and 1 red nutrient to make purple

The field yields a higher output when you match the nutrient colours to the colour of the plant. So orange would be 1 yellow and 1 red nutrient, yellow would be 1 yellow. Doing this should use 15 nutrients per round.
The field yields a higher output when checking each line at a time and then moving on to discern whether or not your mixture is better for the next round. Although there is still some luck involved.

The field yields a higher output when I used the colours (nutrients) I chose made the colour of the plant. For example if the plant was green then I chose the blue and yellow nutrients. If the plant was Yellow I just used the yellow nutrient.
The field yields a higher output when the potions match, or mix to match the flower colour, eg blue and yellow for green flower, or just blue for blue flowers.
The field yields a higher output when you use a single colour per round. For instance, using just blue nutrients in one round. Each nutrient value seems to be around about the same value though.

The field yields a higher output when the you mix the exact primary colors of the plant. for example,   green + yellow = orange (to get a deeper solution, you add +1 red solution); blue + red = purple (add, +1 blue for deeper purple); blue + yellow= green (add +1 more blue). for the primary color solutions, +1 is just right.

The field yields a higher output when the blue circle plant gets +1 blue nutrient. I achieved my highest score when the red plant got +1 red nutrient, all of the orange plants got +1 yellow and red nutrient, the yellow plant got +1 nutrient, the top purple plant gets +1 yellow nutrient, bottom purple plant gets +1 blue and red nutrient, and the green plant got +1 yellow nutrient.

The field yields a higher output when you utilise all of the avaiable 16 points to create as close as possible to en equal number of each colour value. IE; yellow and green make blue, so by using 4 measures of yellow on green, you have two blue. Or by using four measures of blue on red and four measures of red on blue, you have four pourple. I was unable to fully equalise the whole grid, but it also benefits you to use all available 16 points, even if there's still one set of colours which remains uneven. Good luck!

The field yields a higher output when you ignore the combinations that would normally make those colours. For example if you selected both red and yellow or blue and red for all of the purple and orange flowers etc, then selected the primary colours corresponding solution, this would infact give a lower score than a more random combination.
The field yields a higher output when I used blue nutrient and yellow nutrient as the secondary nutrient.
The field yields a higher output when you mix the right colours to get the colour displayed

The field yields a higher output when you make the colour through mixing theory. So orange = 1 red point and 1 yellow point. Blue = 1 blue. Green = 1 blue point and 1 yellow point. Red = 1 red point. Yellow = 1 yellow point.

The field yields a higher output when you spread the nutrients out between all the flowers. A higher number of nutrients for each flower doesn't give a larger score and the colour of the nutrients doesn't seem to make much difference.
The field yields a higher output when you focus on the blue color
The field yields a higher output when you match the colour of the ingredients to the colour of the flower; flowers top seem to have more value in the top left than in the bottom right.
The field yields a higher output when you select 1 for each option of the nutrients provided, randomly distributing the selections. Always choose at least 1 from each item with the different shapes.
The field yields a higher output when you add mixed colours to create a new colour.
The field yields a higher output when you mix the colour of the flower to the ingredients that will make up the colour of the flower.

The field yields a higher output when you give the plants the nutrients with their respective colours, that is, a red plant gets red nutrients. For the mixtures, use the basic colour mixing knowlegde.

The field yields a higher output when you mix the colours of the nutrients accordingly in order to match the colour of the plant. For example, mix red and blue nutrients in order to feed a purple plant. Ensure you use all 16 nutrients. For a colour that directly matches the colour of a nutrient (e.g. the blue plant) you only need to use one nutrient but feel free to use two to ensure all 16 nutrients are used to maximise your reward.
The field yields a higher output when the colours mixed, equal the colour of the plant. For example. 1 blue for a blue plant, or 1 blue and 1 yellow for a green plant.

The field yields a higher output when you give a double blue potion to the blue, then for red give it a single red potion, for orange a single combination of yellow and red, for purple a single combination of red and blue, for green a single combination of blue and yellow, and for yellow a single yellow potion.
The field yields a higher output when you mix the primary colors to get the secondary if you have an idea about colors you will have an edge.
The field yields a higher output when red is used for all, especially the red crop. Red and yellow together makes a higher output on the orange crop.

The field yields a higher output when you prioritize the red nutrient. Also, make a deliberate attempt to double some of your portions. Finally, avoid over-distributing your moves, the more concentrated they are, the higher your points.

The field yields a higher output when you mix the colours that equal the centre of that flower.  For example the green flower will be blue + yellow.  I found that when I used just red for the red flower I only used 15 nutrients, this gave me my lowest score of the game, so I assume that if you don't use all 16 it affects your score. This is why I used blue and yellow on the red flower, this layout me my overall highest score of the game of 900+.

The field yields a higher output when you give 2 of the same colour when it matches the colour of the plant. Then in other scenarios try and do 1 of each of the 2 colours that make the colour of the plant (i.e. blue + yellow = green)

The field yields a higher output when you focus all the inputting something for all the flowers. Additionally, take a stronger focus on the colours that are a mix of two colours for example, Green, Purple. For the single colours blue, red and yellow, focus on adding a solution to the same colour as the flower.

The field yields a higher output when you select the matching colour nutrient for flowers that are primary colours and the nutrient that does NOT make up the flower colour for the flowers that are not primary colours
The field yields a higher output when you mix the nutrients that make the flowers colour for example (red and yellow make orange). A single colour such as blue having just one.

The field yields a higher output when the color of the plant matches the colr of the nutrient i.e. blue plant to blue nutrient,. yellow plant to yellow nutreitn, and red plant to red nutrient. Also, you can mix the color of the nutrient to match the color of the flowers too i.e. mix 1 blue and 1 red nutrient for the purple plant, 1 blue and 2 yellow for the green plant, and 1 yellow and 1 red for the yellowish-reddish plant.

The field yields a higher output when you adherre tocolour theory. I prioritised the single colours over the dual colours, but it is difficult to max out the field. Try and keep to colour theory to keep yield high. I tried experimenting away from this and it never seemed to yield much benefit.

The field yields a higher output when you move quickly and submit quickly, don't spend too much time pondering your answers. I got a higher score when I acted randomly, so that is quite puzzling and I don't reccomend. It seems to like when you add blue, so I added blue to every plant, although I really am not certain if this was the right tactic. There may be an element of colour theory involved with this but I felt under time pressure and wasn't familiar enough with colour theory to implement this in my practice.

The field yields a higher output when you are quick with your selection. I feel that the higher yield comes from selecting colours that equal the plant colour. For example, for a green plant - selecting one blue and one yellow. If the plant is a colour of the nutrient already, I would yield higher by selecting 1 of the same colour. I would not use more than this.
The field yields a higher output when I use less nutrients
The field yields a higher output when you use fewer bottles of the solution.
The field yields a higher output when there is a combination of more red and yellow.

The field yields a higher output when you dont use yellow on orange middle, dont use all potions in 1 round to see if it makes a difference,and take notice of score my highest was around 350 with first bit of advice, good luck
The field yields a higher output when the opposite colours are selected and amounts are messed around with.
The field yields a higher output when not thinking about colour theory - eg don't try and use the nutrients to make the colours of the shapes.

The field yields a higher output when you use the colour that is NOT associated with the colour of the plant. For example, to get green you mix blue and yellow - so you should pick red for this one. For blue, just pick yellow and red. And so on and so forth.
The field yields a higher output when you give each row the same colour and yield. For example, first row got 1 blue each, second row got 1 yellow each. The last row got nothing.
The field yields a higher output when you give the colour a nutrient that is a completely different colour. Just 1. Then use just yellow nutrients leftover for the yellow plant

The field yields a higher output when you match the colours. Some of them require mixing e.g. blue + yellow = green. I would suggest using 2 of the same colour for one of the block colours like blue, red or yellow in order to use all 16 choices.
The field yields a higher output when you match the colors to the plants. Red = Red; green = blue + yellow, etc. Leaving plants without nutirents reduces the score.

The field yields a higher output when you imagine the nutrients as paint, mix them together to create the designated colour eg Green = 1 blue + 1 yellow. However, if the colour of the shape matches the colour of one of the nutrients, add more nutrients to make it stronger eg blue circle = 2/3 blue

The field yields a higher output when you focus on matching the colours, I found you got a higher score when prioritising the colours that do not require mixing to match (e.g. giving a red plant two drops of red instead of focusing on giving orange both red and yellow) however as there is also an element if randomness I guessed a little and made random decisions in places which both increased and decreased my score at times

The field yields a higher output when elements are assigned to properly correspond to the colour of the plant.  For example for a green plant (blue and yellow elements should be chosen) and for a yellow plant (only a yellow element should be chosen). It is advisable to use all 16 elements given, so for the extra element left over, assign it, at random to one of the yellow, blue or red plants.

The field yields a higher output when you mix/select both the primary colours/nutrients (i.e red + blue = purple) to match the correlating colour of the inital big/main plant (which in this case would be purple). When the colour of the plant is a primary colour e.g yellow, i would choose just one nutrient, the yellow one.

The field yields a higher output when you match the nutrient colours to create the colour of the plant (e.g., Blue and yellow to make green, blue and red to make purple, and yellow and red to make orange)
The field yields a higher output when you use the blue nutrients

The field yields a higher output when you mix colours. (red + blue = purple). Do not repeat patterns. (If the shape is orange first use 1 yellow + 1 red; the second orange shape use 2 yellow + 1 red; third use 1 yellow + 2 red). You can leave the primary colours blank in order to allow you more colour patterns.

The field yields a higher output when the colour nutrients that are selected correspond to the colour plant e.g. for an orange plant, I selected one red and one yellow (= orange). However, this seemed to yield higher scores at the beginning of the experiment and decreased as the rounds progressed. For the final 2 rounds, I selected the nutrients completely randomly (but different to previous selections) and yielded the highest results.
The field yields a higher output when you select the nutrient colors needed to make the plant colour. For example, if the plant colour is purple, then select nutrients blue and red.

The field yields a higher output when you spread out the nutrients across all nine squares as evenly as possible, while still using the maximum of 16 nutrients in each round.  I experimented with using less than the maximum 16 nutrients permitted or by concentrating the nutrients on a handful of squares and this resulted in lower outputs. Spread the nutrients out as evenly as possible while still using all 16 nutrients each round.  I was unable to discern any correlation between the colours used and the results.
The field yields a higher output when the nutrients selected combine to make the colour of the flower.
The field yields a higher output when you  nuture youre plants with the right colour

The field yields a higher output when you use 2 of the same colour that makes up the colour of the flower. Eg. 2 blues for a purple flower instead of 1 blue and 1 red or 2 reds for an orange flower instead of 1 yellow and 1 red. The flowers that cannot be made from 2 colours just need 1 colour adding eg. 1 red for a red flower

The field yields a higher output when a primary colour has higher amounts of that colour added to it and when the colours that mix to form the shapes colour are added (e.g. yellow and blue added when the shape is green)

The field yields a higher output when you spread out your nutrients. Stacking them resulted in a low score while the scores remained similar when they were spread. Similar scores were achieved regardless of whether the nutrients added up to make the center colour. (e.g 1 red + 1 blue = purple)
The field yields a higher output when providing nutrients of the same color. When it comes to mixtures, see below: purple = (red + blue nutrients), green = (blue + yellow), orange = (red + yellow)

The field yields a higher output when adding nutrients that make up the the plant (blue for blue, yellow and red for orange), and when choosing the nutrient in order of top first (blue first, yellow, then red) for each plant while going in rows.
The field yields a higher output when patterns are established. based on colours- using the primary colours and seeing what can be made. patterns can be established through repetition.
The field yields a higher output when the nutrients selected match the plant colour or can be mixed to create the plant colour

The field yields a higher output when the nutrient inputted generally match up to the colours used to make the colour matching the plant so red and yellow make orange so I would try get some of the orange plants with those nutrients. The same applied to purple and green though with orange sometimes I would put just yellow or red and increase colours elsewhere to see if that gave greater rewards, for the most part it did

The field yields a higher output when blue is given 1 feed, purple is given blue and red which makes purple. Green is given yellow and blue which makes green. Red is given 1 red. Orange is given yellow and red which makes orange. Yellow is given 1 yellow.

The field yields a higher output when the blue plant has +1 blue nutrient, red plant has +1 blue nutrient and +2 red nutrients, green plant has +1 red nutrient, yellow plant has +1 yellow nutrient, bottom purple has +1 yellow nutrient and +1 red nutrient, middle orange has +1 yellow nutrient and +1 red nutrient, bottom left orange has +2 blue nutrients, bottom right has +2 yellow nutrients
The field yields a higher output when you get as close to the other colours with only using one set of fert
The field yields a higher output when colours are matched. For instance, if the flower is  blue, I found that adding 1 or more blue would increase the output.
The field yields a higher output when you use colours that would not mix together to make the colour of the flower. IE don't use blue and yellow together on the green flower

The field yields a higher output when you put a point in the colours that when mixed creates the colours of the flower. Example; blue flower = 1 blue point. Red flower = 1 red point. Purple flower = 1 blue + 1 red. Green flower = 1 yellow + 1 blue, etc.
The field yields a higher output when you use colour theory to select the potions for the corresponding plant.

The field yields a higher output when mixing the colors together via color theory. IE the purple star flower needs blue and red mixed to make purple for maximum points. The total number of edges seems to have nothing to do with points, nor does the extra point left over from doing the puzzle.

The field yields a higher output when you mix the potions so that they make the intended colour in the drawing. For example, if it was blue, I would just select the blue potion, and if it was purple, I would select one red and one blue, to make purple.
The field yields a higher output when you match the colour of the nutrients to the colours of the flowers, e.g. yellow + red = orange.
The field yields a higher output when you match the flower colour mixed with the nutrients that create that colour.

The field yields a higher output when you match the colour of the solution to the colour of the plant. If there is no matching colour then you mix the 2 primary colours that make that secondary colour.
The field yields a higher output when the options are selected at random

The field yields a higher output when you copy the starting colour on the left. So if it's blue for example just select 1 blue. If its purple add 1 blue and 1 red. Shape doesn't seem to affect the overall values.
The field yields a higher output when you use blue potions
The field yields a higher output when you mathc the colour to the shape for example blue and red = purple, red and yellow = orange, blue and yellow = green etc

The field yields a higher output when you mix the colours to make the colour of the plant, e.g. blue + red for a purple plant, red and yellow for orange plant etc. You can leave the primary coloured plants plain to enable you to use more nutrients for mixing.

The field yields a higher output when you match up the colours. I found it best to use as many of the nutrients as needed, when I used all of them I found my score dropped. I started with this technique, tried a different one for a few trials in the middle but then returned to this technique for my final two trials and found it produced the best result. I tried a more random technique in the middle using less of the nutrients (usually one per plant).

The field yields a higher output when you choose the vials that form the colour of the shape. This is quite a general rule though as I tried to work out whether randomly selecting or selecting colours due to the number of vertices had an impact and I couldn't see a very strong correlation of score with anything. I would say just select randomly.

The field yields a higher output when you match the nutrient colour to the colour which makes up the plant, e.g. blue nutrient for blue, red and yellow for orange etc. In the later stages this seems to change. I experimented with picking the 'wrong' nutrients to see if it was correct and I did manage to attain substantial points, however I could not particularly decipher the pattern in the later stages. I tried to see if the amount of a particular shape or colour present affected the nutrient requirement, in the later stages but again could not find it. I would reccomend initially matching nutrient requirements to the colour of the plant and overall maximising the nutrient availability as evenly as possible across 9 squares.

The field yields a higher output when you focus on the non-primary colors. Create the mixtures and where possible use multiple of a color for a combination. EG for orange do 2 yellow and 1 red. So take priority on the secondary colors.

The field yields a higher output when You create the correct colours used. I tried to make sure all the colours selected would create the colour of the flower. for example yellow square, one red potion. Green heart, One blue and one yellow potion. Purple Star, one Red and one Blue potion. Blue circle, one blue potion.

The field yields a higher output when you feed the plants with matching color e.g match the blue plants with one drop of blue nutrient each, red with one drop of red nutrients each, yellow with one drop of yellow nutrient each. The orange plant gets 2 drops of red nutrients, the purple plants gets 2 drop blue nutrients and the green plant also gets 2 drop of blue nutrients.

The field yields a higher output when nutrients are spread evenly between plants. Trying to match the middle colour to the nutrient colour e.g blue + yellow = green or red and blue = purple proved to further increase the yield.
The field yields a higher output when you add the primary colos to give a secondary color, giving high rewards for the secondary color one have to maximize the element.
The field yields a higher output when you match the flower colour mixed with the nutrients that can create the colour

The field yields a higher output when you go the opposite of what the colour is, or what can be combined to create the colour. As they said in the instructions there is an element of chance, but going against that advice gave me the highest returns (picking the colour based on what isn't that colour of the flower, or what can be combined the make that colour)

The field yields a higher output when you use the colours that make up the shape colours (e.g. blue for blue and red & yellow for orange), feed the plants in order of the rainbow (e.g. red first, then yellow, etc) also chose the nutrients in order of the rainbow (e.g. chose red and then yellow nutrients when making orange).
The field yields a higher output when one uses more yellow points than any other color. combining the blue and red gives high points but using yellow alone gives a higher score than the combination
The field yields a higher output when you use blue

The field yields a higher output when You assign more droplets to yellow orange and apportionate the rest of the droplets between blue and yellow. Although it is very difficult to determine this. Good luck to you.
The field yields a higher output when following the formation shown on the left.
The field yields a higher output when the colours you select mix together to make the colour of the flower. E.g., orange flower - choose red & yellow.
The field yields a higher output when you use the colours of nutrients to create the colour of the flower e.g. blue and red make purple
The field yields a higher output when you include more red and a little bit higher number of blue than yellow.

The field yields a higher output when you use colour theory. If a plant is green then you mix blue and yellow or if a plant is orange, mix red and yellow. Mixing colours seems to yield a higher output compared to plants where they already match a nutrient colour.
The field yields a higher output when you assign multiple of the same colour. For example the blue has more than 1 blue nutrient
The field yields a higher output when you usw the idea for the colour wheel, for example mixing one yellow and one red to give you orange.
The field yields a higher output when feed the feed with the colours of the nuitrients that can make the field colour
The field yields a higher output when  I match colours with nutrients
The field yields a higher output when the colour of the nutrients mixed together or alone match the colour of the flower
The field yields a higher output when I use a mixture of nutrients attimes and also use specific  nutrient which is in line with the colour of the flower.

The field yields a higher output when you use the colours that do not make up the plants colour. If the plant is blue select one each for yellow and red. If the plant is orange, select one for blue as yellow + red = orange so should not be selected.
The field yields a higher output when  you mix the nutrient to match the plants colour.
The field yields a higher output when you mix up the nutrients equally
The field yields a higher output when you mix the colors to make the color of each plant.
The field yields a higher output when you mix all of the colours correctly. ie, purple is 1 blue and 1 red, blue is 1 blue and so on.
The field yields a higher output when you mix the primary colours. Prioritise the crops that are not primary colours

The field yields a higher output when you match the colours to the plant, so for blue 1 blue, purple will be 1 blue + 1 red,  green is 1 blue and 1 yellow, red is 1 red, orange is 1 yellow and 1 red, yellow is 1 yellow. Follow The solution I have done It will provide the max result.
The field yields a higher output when you mix the primary colours together to form the secondary colour. If you know a lot about colour theory you should be able to do it well!

The field yields a higher output when the colours of the nutrients match the colours of the flowers. Red, blue, and yellow should have two of the matching nutrient, and the other colours should have the colours which mix together to match (ie red+blue=purple)
The field yields a higher output when create the color in the flower by using two colors. for the primary colors (red, blue. yellow) choose the option that matches that color.
The field yields a higher output when you focus on purple and green, make sure each color on the field has a nutrient.
The field yields a higher output when you divide the potions equally. Do not let any empty plants.

The field yields a higher output when you make a solution that matches the colour of the plant. Red and yellow for orange, blue and yellow for green, red and blue for purple, and just use the primary colours on their own to match the primary coloured plants.

The field yields a higher output when you mix to colours together that make the colour shown on screen, for example for orange mix a yellow and red inorder to get that orange colour! purple blue and red etc

The field yields a higher output when using a mixture of speed and color theory, matching the colors to what would produce them along with the speed in which you do so. There may also be a connection to the solid color flowers requiring more of the single nutrients than the other flowers that require a mixture.

The field yields a higher output when  colour theory is used to its potential. I beleieve that when using the colour theory and leaving single colours alone it was more successful. Also when completeing the trials quicker i think it also made a difference to my score enhancing ti when i was quickest.

The field yields a higher output when you stick with the correct colours. For an orange flower i used 1 yellow and 1 red, a purple i used 1 blue and 1 red and so on. For colours that dont need mixing i just stuck with the matching colours. This is when i recieved the most points. If i had one nutrient left over i tried giving it to the lightest colour (yellow) and it seemed to work well.
The field yields a higher output when you use less than 3 nutrents for each plant, and no more than one of each kind - they seem to favour red + yellow
The field yields a higher output when yellow and red are the predominant colours
The field yields a higher output when you use the nutritent colours which make the colour in the plants, but mainly for red and yellow nutrients
The field yields a higher output when you dont miss the same colours on the filed also try not to use yellow on orange middle.
The field yields a higher output when you consider primary and secondary colors. This would help you drop required nutrients.

The field yields a higher output when you only use BLUE. Select blue twice for the first 7 plants, followed by 1 blue on the last 2. In other words 2/2/2/2/2/2/2/1/1. But remember only use blue.

The field yields a higher output when fed with blue. Only use blue and use all 16 nutrients. Give 2 blue to the first seven plants and 1 blue to the last two plants. At best this gave me 880 points and at worst 680.
The field yields a higher output when you match up each plant symbol together so they recieve the same treatment.

The field yields a higher output when have the same amount of input on the same shapes and never have all 3 inputs on one shape as it will lower the score. you have 6 attempts to work out the best method and add onto what i found out in my experment.
The field yields a higher output when you double up on the colours.
The field yields a higher output when you don't mix different colours and there are more blue than red or yellow used.
The field yields a higher output when select a single colour for each plant picking more of blue and a bit of yellow.
The field yields a higher output when avoiding reds
The field yields a higher output when you use a single nutrient for each plant. You don't have to use a certain type of nutrients but it works better in the field if you only use one per plant.

The field yields a higher output when blue is chosen for the field generally. however from my experience certain plants yield more when given different nutrients for example the star plant i tried giving 2 blue instead and i tried 1 blue and 1 red and the latter is more similarly i tried to give 2 blue nutrients to the plus plant and that gave me a lower score; i think theres a link between the nutrients to the shapes of the plants. if you can figure out the star plant and plus plant then you can figure out what yields the highest score i recommend giving at least one blue to all the other plants. and see how they do. i averaged about 600 for each round the person before me had a score of 859; the only thing i changed in my solution was i gave an extra blue to the plus instead of a yellow to the circle. otherwise we have the same solution
The field yields a higher output when using blue and yellow combined. Keeping values at 1 is best.
The field yields a higher output when there is more of red. The red nutrient is the most important so let it occur most. Give the central flower at least one of each nutrient. All the best
The field yields a higher output when you feed your plant more of red than other colours

The field yields a higher output when you make the following selection across each row, from left to right; 1 blue, 2 yellow, 1 red; Second row: 1 blue, 1 yellow, 1 red; Third row: 2 blue, 1 yellow, 2 red
The field yields a higher output when at least one blue nutrient is used for each of the plants.
The field yields a higher output when you only use blue.
The field yields a higher output when +1 blue is added to each
The field yields a higher output when selecting blue for each plant. Other mixes seem to decrease the points.
The field yields a higher output when you use blue potions except for the flowers with a plus in the middle

The field yields a higher output when there is blue involved. Always add blue to each flower. I tried adding both red and yellow along with the blue, and red almost always gave me a higher output, besides in one round. However, a yellow and blue combination gave me an okay output. So, I would recommend doing a combination of red and blue on most flowers but make sure to always add blue. I found that combinations did better than just blue alone.
The field yields a higher output when you add the total of nutrients used to 16, and also just using blue and yellow scores higher points together when red is not involved.
The field yields a higher output when each plant is fed equally and rows/columns of of plants all have a different colour.
The field yields a higher output when the nutrients are distributed equally across the field. When doing so, take note of the field with similar shapes and reduce their nutrients.
The field yields a higher output when you use all the 16 nutrients and make use of more of yellow and blue nutrients.

The field yields a higher output when you distribute the nutrients more evenly, focus on getting blue on every field, having yellow as your second most frequent nutrient but having a couple of fields with all three nutrients.
The field yields a higher output when you use all 16 nutrients in a round and also make use of yellow and blue nutrients only, no plant should be left without.

The field yields a higher output when you feed them blue nutrients and ensure the number of nutrients used is even, blue and yellow nutrients gives better result than blue and yelloe, there should always be blue for each plant.

The field yields a higher output when you do the same thing for each pattern, and when you only use one colour for each pattern. I found the score was negatively impacted when using more than one colour for each pattern.
The field yields a higher output when you match the patterns to the amount of nutrients you use
The field yields a higher output when choose the yellow solution.
The field yields a higher output when you try different solutions. Spread numbers across the board
The field yields a higher output when you spread the nutrients out and use higher numbers than lower (i.e. 2 and 3 instead of 1)
The field yields a higher output when you select only nutrients on the plants. I found success in using at least one on each plant then choosing a selection to add an additional nutrient to.
The field yields a higher output when you use more yellow nutrient than other colors. I spread 2 yellow nutrients in many fields and 1 yellow in some. In one field, i used 2 blue and 1 red

The field yields a higher output when You match the same shapes with the same colour combinations, yellow works best solo and not paired with other colours, but make sure to swtich it up with other combinations such as red and blue otherwise the yeild will decrease.
The field yields a higher output when you use the blue nutrients and spread all 16 across all 9 plants
The field yields a higher output when you spread potions around the yield
The field yields a higher output when you give each flower one dosage across the board, then follow a pattern of every second flower a dosage, moving down the colour board.

The field yields a higher output when at least all plants have a drop of blue. The most output I was able to receive was 850ish and when all the plants had a drop of blue and the only repeating patterrns, the star and plus sigh, I added a drop of yellow to only those.
The field yields a higher output when blue is used in every one, some may require a yellow added to them but most of them are solely just blue.

The field yields a higher output when you use most, if not all of the nutrients available. I acted on the advice that yellow and blue yeilds the most points, and in one of my rounds I used the red nutrient on several plants, and yielded the lowest points in that round. I assume the red has a negative impact on your score. I also made sure that every plant was fed some kind of nutrient.
The field yields a higher output when you provide at least one red to each flower, making sure the middle flower has at least 2-4 reds and 2-4 of each of the other colors.

The field yields a higher output when , the red alone seemed to offer the most output(1 red on each plant). Closely followed by blue(1 blue on each). Yellow seemed to offer the least value(1 yellow on each). Perhaps play around with the red and blue in varying combinationts. Good luck
The field yields a higher output when theren is more of red and yellow. The red has more nutrient content.
The field yields a higher output when at least one blue nutrient is added to each of the plants.
The field yields a higher output when you put +1 blue in all and +1 yellow in 3
The field yields a higher output when all the plants have at least one blue

The field yields a higher output when you start by combining red and blue then as you progress, you introduce an equal distribution of red and yellow combined with blue. Blue appears to be the strongest.

The field yields a higher output when you use the BLUE potions. But using a MIXTURE of BLUE and RED potions give the highest points EXCEPT! from the flowers with the PLUS/CROSS shapes in the centre. For these I tried -only blue - double blue. Only blue worked best. I used 15 nutrients across each field.  Blue and Yellow give a better output than yellow and red, but neither as high as BLUE and RED.     Do's - ALWAYS USE BLUE. - USE BLUE AND RED ON EACH FLOWER   Dont's - DO NOT USE RED ON THE + SHAPED FLOWERS, Blue alone will give the higher yield.
The field yields a higher output when you always add blue. Try to double up where you can. Mixing red with at least 1 of the blues seems to give a bit of a boost
The field yields a higher output when matching the three in a row or column.
The field yields a higher output when shapes that are repeated have less nutrient and the rows/columns of plants have a different colour.
The field yields a higher output when the same colour feed is given to all of the plants, with the same center shapes being given the same quantity.

The field yields a higher output when you distribute the rounder shapes unevenly and more square shapes evenly. rounder shapes will usually only need blue or yellow, more square shapes should be even. Dont add more than 2 of 1 colour or 3 total on a flower
The field yields a higher output when distribute them evenly, but let your focus be extensively on blue and yellow to gain more points

The field yields a higher output when you use the blue solution in each of your plants. The second most used should then be yellow, and lastly read. The solutions should be spread in different plants though.
The field yields a higher output when you use blue over the other colours , I would try and use even numbers as this appears to be higher , I would not use red as this provided a lower output
The field yields a higher output when nutrients are mixed with blue and yellow. It seemed that red nutrients deprecated the output of points.

The field yields a higher output when you feed them blue nutrients and ensure you maintain same number across board, e.g. one blue nutrient for all. It also performed well when I added yellow nutrients as opposed to red nutrients
The field yields a higher output when it changes every round. I submitted the same solution for three rounds and got different results.
The field yields a higher output when solutions are mixed e.g. 1 red and 1 blue and they are spread across the field not just close together
The field yields a higher output when you keep at least one yellow nutrient in each per time. Alternate between blue and red each time in each box.

The field yields a higher output when you select only yellow nutrients on the plant however; make sure that you switch it up with other combinations between rounds I.e. 1 yellow, 1 red on one plant, then next round, only 2 yellow.

The field yields a higher output when you use the same exact amount of nutrients for the ones that have matching shapes. The yellow seems to do better on the ones that do not have a match for their shape.
The field yields a higher output when you put 2 nutrients on the middle and botton row of the field. I find the yellow and red work better than the blue one.
The field yields a higher output when you use only blue potions for the first 8 flowers (so 2 blue per flower for the first 8)
The field yields a higher output when you slipt your available chances mostly on blue and yellow
The field yields a higher output when you spread it across as much as possible prioritizing the same crops.
The field yields a higher output when you use more yellow for the crosses. It also possibly responds better when you use all the nutrients.

The field yields a higher output when I gave all 1 blue and then all yellow for crosses like my solution. I did my solution twice (keeping most of the yellow on the left cross) and got 700-800 points each time.
The field yields a higher output when you use all 16 nutrients and stars and pluses are given 1 yellow nutrient, and the remaining shapes are given a combination of nutrients.
The field yields a higher output when the blue nutrient is used evenly across the board. A combination of red and blue is effective as well, and not all 16 nutrients need to be used in a round.
The field yields a higher output when blue is spread evenly across the field and red is also given to all the plus flowers.

The field yields a higher output when you use the blue one evenly, seemingly the other colours lower the score, and using them together makes the score lower. The highest score I got was from using one blue on each one, and no other colours. I got 930 on that one
The field yields a higher output when you use the blue option. I think the square and triangle give off the most points.

The field yields a higher output when you prioritise red and yellow solutions. I was achieving the best outcomes on average using these two colours. Even though the results were different each time, the consistency of this method was the most beneficial.

The field yields a higher output when you add 1 red and 1 yellow nutrient to the plants on the top two horizontal rows and then on the final row adding one red or one yellow nutrient to each plant and another final nutrient to either red or yellow  to a plant of your choice on that row.
The field yields a higher output when you focus your points distribution on the bottom row then focus on red and yellow for the middle and then distribute yellow and blue for the top.

The field yields a higher output when you only do Blue and yellow on the top and middle row. Then the bottom row you have to do Blue and yellow on the first plant then only yellow then only red. Happy feeding!
The field yields a higher output when red and yellow are combined in the top section
The field yields a higher output when you use yellows and blue. I noticed that the reds yeild a lower output.
The field yields a higher output when you mix blue and yellow and disperse a red nutrient around the field.
The field yields a higher output when you add 1 blue and 1 yellow to each plant in order to maximize your total score.
The field yields a higher output when yellow is used instead of blue
The field yields a higher output when you add yellow to each flower and use the remainder to add red.

The field yields a higher output when you give every plant at least 1 yellow. I had success when giving each one yellow, and allocating the remaining red to each one - you will not be able to give each one a red as you will max out. I scored highly when allocating only 15 and not 16 of the nutrients.
The field yields a higher output when you select 1 blue nutrient for each plant and 1 yellow nutrient for the star and cross plants.
The field yields a higher output when you cover every flower with a blue. the the rest with red and yellow, I couldn't see much logic beyond that.
The field yields a higher output when there is one blue for each, one red for all the stars, and one or two yellows for plus symbols.

The field yields a higher output when you select the blue nutrient. I don't know what but I have noticed that the symbols in the middle of the flower play some role. Flowers with the same symbol should have the same amount of nutrients e.g. both stars are 1 and all crosses are 2.

The field yields a higher output when you use only blue nutrients to feed the plants. Use all 16 nutrients-give 2 blues to the first 7 plants, and then 1 blue each to the remaining two plants for a total of 16.
The field yields a higher output when you give 1 blue to first 2 plants and then 2 blue to rest of plants. Only use blue.
The field yields a higher output when you give the same treatment to plants of the same shape.

The field yields a higher output when you use same amount of nutrients for same shape of plants and also use remaining nutrients as per the shape placement as in use middle nutrient for middle line plant.
The field yields a higher output when you give all plants one or two blue nutrients and nothing else. Use all 16 nutrients on blue.

The field yields a higher output when you avoid red entirely and only use blue and yellow. Use more blue than yellow, focusing especially on the first row. Also evenly spread out the blue and yellow making sure every shape has a colour. Don't put too much colour in one shape as this will yield in less points.
The field yields a higher output when select a single colour for each picking more of blue and a bit of yellow
The field yields a higher output when you use mostly blue with a bit of yellow sprinkled in
